blocks.original_text: “The Watch has no shortage of stableboys,” Lord Mormont grumbled. “That seems to be all they send us these days. Stableboys and sneak thieves and rapers. blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont抱怨Watch只收到stableboys、thieves和rapers，因此仍需少有的anointed knight。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont的反驳不是说Ser Alliser优秀，而是组织没有替代者；稀缺性保护了低质量权威。
blocks.background_zh: **招募：** 当前Watch接收大量贫穷者和罪犯，受过正式骑士训练者极少，因此Ser Alliser即使教学恶劣仍难替代。

blocks.original_text: “On the wrong side,” Ser Jaremy Rykker commented dryly. “I ought to know, I was there on the battlements beside him. Tywin Lannister gave us a splendid choice. Take the black, or see our heads on spikes before evenfall. No offense intended, Tyrion.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jaremy Rykker指出Ser Alliser为败方作战，Tywin让他们在take black和斩首间选择。
blocks.literary_note_zh: take the black在此是战败者避免处决的选择，说明Watch人才也来自政治清算。
blocks.background_zh: **历史：** Ser Alliser与Jaremy曾在King’s Landing为Targaryen一方守城；Tywin破城后让他们take black或被处死。

blocks.original_text: “None taken, Ser Jaremy. My father is very fond of spiked heads, especially those of people who have annoyed him in some fashion. blocks.original_text: Mormont frowned through his thick grey beard. “Snow? Oh, the Stark bastard. I think not. The young ones need to forget the lives they left behind them, the brothers and mothers and all that. A visit home would only stir up feelings best left alone. I know these things. My own blood kin . . . my sister Maege rules Bear Island now, since my son’s dishonor. I have nieces I have never seen.” He took a swallow. “Besides, Jon Snow is only a boy. You shall have three strong swords, to keep you safe.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont拒绝，认为新兵须忘旧家；又提自己因儿子dishonor远离Bear Island亲族。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont以自身亲族断裂说明forget旧生活不是只要求新兵承受的规则。
blocks.background_zh: **Mormont家族：** Jeor的妹妹Maege治理Bear Island；其son因dishonor离开，具体经过本段未说明。

blocks.original_text: “I am touched by your concern, Lord Mormont.” The strong drink was making Tyrion light-headed, but not so drunk that he did not realize that the Old Bear wanted something from him. “I hope I can repay your kindness.”

blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion看穿gift后面的request；政治交换不因发生在Wall便消失。

blocks.original_text: “You can,” Mormont said bluntly. “Your sister sits beside the king. Your brother is a great knight, and your father the most powerful lord in the Seven Kingdoms. Speak to them for us. Tell them of our need here. You have seen for yourself, my lord. The Night’s Watch is dying. Our strength is less than a thousand now. Six hundred here, two hundred in the Shadow Tower, even fewer at Eastwatch, and a scant third of those fighting men. The Wall is a hundred leagues long. Think on that. Should an attack come, I have three men to defend each mile of wall.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont要他向Cersei、Jaime和Tywin求援，并用不足千人、每英里三人说明Watch将死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 三人守一英里是平均数，却强烈显出一百league防线与不足千人的结构性不可能。
blocks.background_zh: **兵力：** Mormont称全Watch不足千人，约六百在Castle Black、二百Shadow Tower、Eastwatch更少，且仅三分之一能战斗。

blocks.original_text: “Three and a third,” Tyrion said with a yawn.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion用three and a third纠正数字。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont用护送换取政治求援，说明Watch人力、领导与防线危机，并把long winter与北方异常连成警告。

blocks.original_text: Mormont scarcely seemed to hear him. The old man warmed his hands before the fire. “I sent Benjen Stark to search after Yohn Royce’s son, lost on his first ranging. The Royce boy was green as summer grass, yet he insisted on the honor of his own command, saying it was his due as a knight. I did not wish to offend his lord father, so I yielded. I sent him out with two men I deemed as good as any in the Watch. More fool I.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont承认因Waymar Royce骑士身份而让其首次ranging领队，后悔向贵族父亲让步。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Waymar的knight身份使Mormont让渡专业判断，贵族礼仪在边境造成实际风险。
blocks.background_zh: **前文：** Ser Waymar Royce第一次ranging便以knight身份获得指挥，随后失踪；Benjen正为搜索其队伍而未归。

blocks.original_text: “Fool,” the raven agreed. Tyrion glanced up. The bird peered down at him with those beady black eyes, ruffling its wings. “Fool,” it called again. Doubtless old Mormont would take it amiss if he throttled the creature. A pity.
blocks.explanation_zh: raven重复Fool，Tyrion忍住掐它的念头。

blocks.original_text: The Lord Commander took no notice of the irritating bird. “Gared was near as old as I am and longer on the Wall,” he went on, “yet it would seem he forswore himself and fled. I should never have believed it, not of him, but Lord Eddard sent me his head from Winterfell. Of Royce, there is no word. One deserter and two men lost, and now Ben Stark too has gone missing.” He sighed deeply. “Who am I to send searching after him? In two years I will be seventy. Too old and too weary for the burden I bear, yet if I set it down, who will pick it up? Alliser Thorne? Bowen Marsh? I would have to be as blind as Maester Aemon not to see what they are. The Night’s Watch has become an army of sullen boys and tired old men. Apart from the men at my table tonight, I have perhaps twenty who can read, and even fewer who can think, or plan, or lead. Once the Watch spent its summers building, and each Lord Commander raised the Wall higher than he found it. Now it is all we can do to stay alive.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说Gared逃亡被Eddard处死、Waymar失踪、Benjen也未归；Watch缺读写、思考和领导人才。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 从曾经增高Wall到如今勉强存活，组织衰退不仅是人数少，也是识字、规划和领导能力流失。
blocks.background_zh: **事实链：** Gared逃到南方被Eddard依法处决并送头；Waymar及另一同伴失踪，Benjen搜索队也逾期。

blocks.original_text: He was in deadly earnest, Tyrion realized. He felt faintly embarrassed for the old man. Lord Mormont had spent a good part of his life on the Wall, and he needed to believe if those years were to have any meaning. “I promise, the king will hear of your need,” Tyrion said gravely, “and I will speak to my father and my brother Jaime as well.” And he would. Tyrion Lannister was as good as his word. He left the rest unsaid; that King Robert would ignore him, Lord Tywin would ask if he had taken leave of his senses, and Jaime would only laugh.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion认为Mormont必须相信一生牺牲有意义，仍真诚承诺转达，但心知Robert、Tywin、Jaime都不会响应。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion既判断Mormont以信念支撑人生，又仍履行承诺；怀疑对方心理不等于背弃自己的word。

blocks.original_text: “As you say, my lord.” He had been born in the dead of winter, a terrible cruel one that the maesters said had lasted near three years, but Tyrion’s earliest memories were of spring.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion想起自己生于近三年的严冬，却最早只记得spring。

blocks.original_text: “When I was a boy, it was said that a long summer always meant a long winter to come. This summer has lasted nine years, Tyrion, and a tenth will soon be upon us. Think on that.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说九年long summer预示同样漫长的winter。
blocks.literary_note_zh: long summer／long winter是经验性民间判断；九年夏季使未来冬季成为制度规划问题。
blocks.background_zh: **季节：** Westeros季节长度不固定；当前summer已九年，Citadel观测显示将结束。

blocks.original_text: “When I was a boy,” Tyrion replied, “my wet nurse told me that one day, if men were good, the gods would give the world a summer without ending. Perhaps we’ve been better than we thought, and the Great Summer is finally at hand.” He grinned.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion用奶妈所说endless summer反讽，也许人类表现太好。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont用护送换取政治求援，说明Watch人力、领导与防线危机，并把long winter与北方异常连成警告。

blocks.original_text: The Lord Commander did not seem amused. “You are not fool enough to believe that, my lord. Already the days grow shorter. There can be no mistake, Aemon has had letters from the Citadel, findings in accord with his own. The end of summer stares us in the face.” Mormont reached out and clutched Tyrion tightly by the hand. “You must make them understand. I tell you, my lord, the darkness is coming. There are wild things in the woods, direwolves and mammoths and snow bears the size of aurochs, and I have seen darker shapes in my dreams.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont引用Citadel与Aemon观测确认summer将终，紧握Tyrion说darkness正来、野兽和梦中黑影出现。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mormont把Citadel观测、野兽活动、梦境混在一起；证据强度不同，但共同构成他的紧迫感。

blocks.original_text: “Denys Mallister writes that the mountain people are moving south, slipping past the Shadow Tower in numbers greater than ever before. They are running, my lord . . . but running from what?” Lord Mormont moved to the window and stared out into the night. “These are old bones, Lannister, but they have never felt a chill like this. Tell the king what I say, I pray you. Winter is coming, and when the Long Night falls, only the Night’s Watch will stand between the realm and the darkness that sweeps from the north. The gods help us all if we are not ready.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mormont说山民大量南逃，并问他们在逃离什么；Long Night来时只有Night’s Watch挡住北方黑暗。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 山民南迁是可观察行为，‘逃什么’仍是推断；Mormont把不确定迹象组织成单一北方威胁。
blocks.background_zh: **观察／推断：** mountain people南移由Denys报告；他们是否因超自然威胁逃亡仍未知。

blocks.original_text: “The gods help me if I do not get some sleep tonight. Yoren is determined to ride at first light.” Tyrion got to his feet, sleepy from wine and tired of doom. “I thank you for all the courtesies you have done me, Lord Mormont.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion困倦厌烦doom，起身道谢并说Yoren天亮出发。

blocks.original_text: “Tell them, Tyrion. Tell them and make them believe. That is all the thanks I need.” He whistled, and his raven flew to him and perched on his shoulder. Mormont smiled and gave the bird some corn from his pocket, and that was how Tyrion left him.

blocks.original_text: It was bitter cold outside. Bundled thickly in his furs, Tyrion Lannister pulled on his gloves and nodded to the poor frozen wretches standing sentry outside the Commander’s Keep. He set off across the yard for his own chambers in the King’s Tower, walking as briskly as his legs could manage. Patches of snow crunched beneath his feet as his boots broke the night’s crust, and his breath steamed before him like a banner. He shoved his hands into his armpits and walked faster, praying that Morrec had remembered to warm his bed with hot bricks from the fire.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion出门承受苦寒，快速走向King’s Tower并盼热砖暖床。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion放弃暖床登上Wall；高空亲眼见到Castle Black衰败、维护困难和被遗忘的防御器械。

blocks.original_text: Behind the King’s Tower, the Wall glimmered in the light of the moon, immense and mysterious. Tyrion stopped for a moment to look up at it. His legs ached of cold and haste.

blocks.original_text: Suddenly a strange madness took hold of him, a yearning to look once more off the end of the world. It would be his last chance, he thought; tomorrow he would ride south, and he could not imagine why he would ever want to return to this frozen desolation. The King’s Tower was before him, with its promise of warmth and a soft bed, yet Tyrion found himself walking past it, toward the vast pale palisade of the Wall.
blocks.explanation_zh: 临行前他突然渴望再看一次世界尽头，放弃眼前温床走向Wall。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 温床与Wall分岔，Tyrion选择无实际收益的好奇，体现他无法只接受二手叙述。

blocks.original_text: A wooden stair ascended the south face, anchored on huge rough-hewn beams sunk deep into the ice and frozen in place. Back and forth it switched, clawing its way upward as crooked as a bolt of lightning. The black brothers assured him that it was much stronger than it looked, but Tyrion’s legs were cramping too badly for him to even contemplate the ascent. He went instead to the iron cage beside the well, clambered inside, and yanked hard on the bell rope, three quick pulls.
blocks.explanation_zh: 木楼梯太难攀，他进入iron cage并拉铃求升。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion放弃暖床登上Wall；高空亲眼见到Castle Black衰败、维护困难和被遗忘的防御器械。
blocks.background_zh: **升降设施：** Castle Black用木梯和winch cage登Wall；Tyrion因腿部状况选择cage。

blocks.original_text: He had to wait what seemed an eternity, standing there inside the bars with the Wall to his back. Long enough for Tyrion to begin to wonder why he was doing this. He had just about decided to forget his sudden whim and go to bed when the cage gave a jerk and began to ascend.

The Old Bear had more sense than he did, it seemed.

blocks.original_text: Then he was above the towers, still inching his way upward. Castle Black lay below him, etched in moonlight. You could see how stark and empty it was from up here; windowless keeps, crumbling walls, courtyards choked with broken stone. Farther off, he could see the lights of Mole’s Town, the little village half a league south along the kingsroad, and here and there the bright glitter of moonlight on water where icy streams descended from the mountain heights to cut across the plains. The rest of the world was a bleak emptiness of windswept hills and rocky fields spotted with snow.
blocks.explanation_zh: 高空月色显示Castle Black空旷破败，远处Mole’s Town和水光之外尽是荒凉。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 俯瞰把Mormont所说衰败变成窗空、墙塌、庭院碎石的亲眼证据。
blocks.background_zh: **地理：** Mole’s Town在Castle Black以南约半league，是依附驻地的小村落。

blocks.original_text: Finally a thick voice behind him said, “Seven hells, it’s the dwarf,” and the cage jerked to a sudden stop and hung there, swinging slowly back and forth, the ropes creaking.

blocks.original_text: “Bring him in, damn it.” There was a grunt and a loud groaning of wood as the cage slid sideways and then the Wall was beneath him. Tyrion waited until the swinging had stopped before he pushed open the cage door and hopped down onto the ice. A heavy figure in black was leaning on the winch, while a second held the cage with a gloved hand. Their faces were muffled in woolen scarves so only their eyes showed, and they were plump with layers of wool and leather, black on black. “And what will you be wanting, this time of night?” the one by the winch asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: 工人横移cage让Tyrion踏上Wall，询问深夜来意。

blocks.original_text: It was bitingly cold up here, and the wind pulled at his clothes like an insistent lover. The top of the Wall was wider than the kingsroad often was, so Tyrion had no fear of falling, although the footing was slicker than he would have liked. The brothers spread crushed stone across the walkways, but the weight of countless footsteps would melt the Wall beneath, so the ice would seem to grow around the gravel, swallowing it, until the path was bare again and it was time to crush more stone.
blocks.explanation_zh: Wall顶极宽却冰滑；脚步融冰吞没碎石，需要反复铺设。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Wall会吞没防滑gravel，维护劳动永无完成；巨大奇迹依赖反复细小工作。
blocks.background_zh: **维护：** Wall顶铺crushed stone防滑，但脚步融冰会吞没石料，因此需持续重铺。

blocks.original_text: Still, it was nothing that Tyrion could not manage. He looked off to the east and west, at the Wall stretching before him, a vast white road with no beginning and no end and a dark abyss on either side. West, he decided, for no special reason, and he began to walk that way, following the pathway nearest the north edge, where the gravel looked freshest.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion选西行，Wall像无始无终的白路，两侧是黑暗深渊。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion放弃暖床登上Wall；高空亲眼见到Castle Black衰败、维护困难和被遗忘的防御器械。

blocks.original_text: His bare cheeks were ruddy with the cold, and his legs complained more loudly with every step, but Tyrion ignored them. The wind swirled around him, gravel crunched beneath his boots, while ahead the white ribbon followed the lines of the hills, rising higher and higher, until it was lost beyond the western horizon. He passed a massive catapult, as tall as a city wall, its base sunk deep into the Wall. The throwing arm had been taken off for repairs and then forgotten; it lay there like a broken toy, half-embedded in the ice.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他忍住脸腿寒痛，经过被遗忘维修、半埋冰中的巨型catapult。
blocks.literary_note_zh: broken toy与Wall宏伟相冲突，废弃catapult证明防御设施也受人力衰退侵蚀。

blocks.original_text: The Night’s Watch permitted the forest to come no closer than half a mile of the north face of the Wall. The thickets of ironwood and sentinel and oak that had once grown there had been harvested centuries ago, to create a broad swath of open ground through which no enemy could hope to pass unseen. Tyrion had heard that elsewhere along the Wall, between the three fortresses, the wildwood had come creeping back over the decades, that there were places where grey-green sentinels and pale white weirwoods had taken root in the shadow of the Wall itself, but Castle Black had a prodigious appetite for firewood, and here the forest was still kept at bay by the axes of the black brothers.
blocks.explanation_zh: Watch清理Wall北面半英里林带，但三堡之间许多地方森林已重新逼近。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人并肩面对haunted forest；Tyrion的讽刺确定性松动，Jon则发誓若Benjen不归便深入黑暗寻找。
blocks.background_zh: **防御林带：** Castle Black北侧保留半英里开阔地防敌隐蔽接近；无人堡段的forest正重新逼近Wall。

blocks.original_text: It was never far, though. From up here Tyrion could see it, the dark trees looming beyond the stretch of open ground, like a second wall built parallel to the first, a wall of night. Few axes had ever swung in that black wood, where even the moonlight could not penetrate the ancient tangle of root and thorn and grasping limb. Out there the trees grew huge, and the rangers said they seemed to brood and knew not men. It was small wonder the Night’s Watch named it the haunted forest.
blocks.explanation_zh: haunted forest像与冰墙平行的night wall，古老密林几乎不透月光。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forest作为第二道wall与人造冰墙平行：一边由人划定，另一边由古老黑暗构成。

blocks.original_text: As he stood there and looked at all that darkness with no fires burning anywhere, with the wind blowing and the cold like a spear in his guts, Tyrion Lannister felt as though he could almost believe the talk of the Others, the enemy in the night. His jokes of grumkins and snarks no longer seemed quite so droll.
blocks.explanation_zh: 面对无火黑暗和刺骨寒风，Tyrion几乎相信Others，旧日grumkins笑话不再好笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 环境体验没有证明Others，却改变了Tyrion对传说的可相信程度；这是情感认知变化而非事实确认。
blocks.background_zh: **事实／感受：** Tyrion在环境压力下‘几乎相信’Others，但本段没有新增直接目击。

blocks.original_text: “My uncle is out there,” Jon Snow said softly, leaning on his spear as he stared off into the darkness. “The first night they sent me up here, I thought, Uncle Benjen will ride back tonight, and I’ll see him first and blow the horn. He never came, though. Not that night and not any night.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jon说Benjen在黑暗中；每次值夜都盼第一个看见他归来，却从未实现。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 值夜原本是惩罚，Jon却赋予它等待Benjen的私人意义。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Watch制度衰退、语言与权威、Tyrion的观察、Jon的成长或北方威胁证据强度。

blocks.original_text: “Give him time,” Tyrion said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion劝他再给叔叔时间。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 两人并肩面对haunted forest；Tyrion的讽刺确定性松动，Jon则发誓若Benjen不归便深入黑暗寻找。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Watch制度衰退、语言与权威、Tyrion的观察、Jon的成长或北方威胁证据强度。

blocks.original_text: Far off to the north, a wolf began to howl. Another voice picked up the call, then another. Ghost cocked his head and listened. “If he doesn’t come back,” Jon Snow promised, “Ghost and I will go find him.” He put his hand on the direwolf’s head.
blocks.explanation_zh: 远方狼嚎接力，Jon按住Ghost发誓若Benjen不回便亲自寻找。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 狼嚎从单声变合声，映衬Jon与Ghost的结盟，也让北方黑暗显得有回应。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Watch制度衰退、语言与权威、Tyrion的观察、Jon的成长或北方威胁证据强度。

blocks.original_text: “I believe you,” Tyrion said, but what he thought was, And who will go find you? He shivered.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion口头相信，心中却问届时谁去找Jon，并发冷。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “who will go find you”把忠诚誓言反转成失踪循环，结尾寒意既来自天气也来自预感。
